latest.title: New Loan Facility and Operational Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 3540M
Kropz PLC
15 July 2026
The information contained within this announcement is deemed to constitute inside information as stipulated under the retained EU law version of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No. 596/2014 (the "
UK MAR
") which is part of UK law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018. The information is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of the UK MAR. Upon the publication of this announcement, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
15 July 2026
Kropz Plc
("
Kropz
" or the "
Company
")
New Loan FacilityandOperational Update
Kropz Plc (AIM: KRPZ), an emerging African phosphate producer and developer, announces that Kropz Elandsfontein (Pty) Ltd ("
Elandsfontein
")
and Ubunto-Botho Investments (Pty) Ltd
("
UBI
")
(together the "
Parties
") have agreed to a ZAR 200 million (approximately US$ 12.3 million) (the "
Loan
"), which constitutes a related party transaction.
Operational Update
The Elandsfontein mine continues to progress through its production ramp-up phase. As previously announced, record monthly production was achieved in March with over 40,000 tonnes of production. However, mining operations continue to be materially affected by variability in the ore body, including the nature and volume of slimes material, hard bank and pink ore encountered within the deposit. These conditions have resulted in slower mining rates and lower concentrate output in the quarter ended 30 June 2026. Production was also disrupted in the quarter due to availability of the mining contractor, which interrupted ore flow to the processing plant and constrained the operational flexibility required to sustain more stable production levels.
Geopolitical tensions have created additional commercial headwinds with higher energy and consumable costs, and an increase in freight rates.
Elandsfontein produced 95 956 tonnes of phosphate concentrate in quarter ended 30 June 2026 (115,686: quarter to 31 March 2026) representing a 17% decrease compared quarter to quarter. Sales for the period totalled 183,714 tonnes, and stock at the quarter end was higher than anticipated being approximately 94,000 tonnes.
The operational issues, combined with the market factors described above, has given rise to a further working capital funding requirement. Consequently, the Company has entered into the Loan with UBI to support the group.
The Loan will be used by Elandsfontein to fund its cashflow and operational expenditure needs.
The Loan
Elandsfontein and UBI have agreed terms for a loan facility of ZAR 200 million (approximately US$ 12.3 million). Interest is payable on the Loan at the South African prime overdraft interest rate plus 6%, nominal per annum and compounded monthly. The Loan is repayable on the earlier of a date as agreed between the Parties or on demand from UBI, on no less than 15 business days' notice. There is no fixed term.
As announced on 21 May 2025 and 28 May 2025, Kropz SA (Pty) Ltd has issued a Guarantee, Security Cession and Pledge in favour of the ARC Fund (
ARC
), and Kropz Plc has issued a Limited Indemnity, Security Cession and Pledge in favour of ARC for all current and future loan facilities made to Elandsfontein, which are applicable to the Loan.These agreements will be extended by the parties in favour of UBI as the first ranking priority (through an Omnibus agreement), and ARC and the second ranking priority issued by Kropz SA (Pty) Ltd for all current and future loan facilities of Kropz Elandsfontein.
The Company will update the market on drawdown of the Loan in due course.
Related Party Transaction
UBI is the indirect controlling shareholder of ARC, and consequently the Loan and the Omnibus agreement are related party transactions pursuant to Rule 13 of the AIM Rules for Companies. Gerrit Duminy, a director of the Company, is the representative of ARC. Accordingly, Gerrit Duminy has not been involved in the approval of the Loan by the Company's board. The directors of the Company, who are considered independent for the purposes of the Loan, having consulted with the Company's nominated adviser, consider the terms of the Loan to be fair and reasonable insofar as the Company's shareholders are concerned.
For further information visit
www.kropz.com
or contact:
Kropz Plc

About Kropz Plc
Kropz is an emerging African phosphate producer and developer with projects in South Africa and in the Republic of Congo. The vision of the Group is to become a leading independent phosphate rock producer and to develop into an integrated, mine-to-market plant nutrient company focusing on sub-Saharan Africa.

Total Voting Rights
Kropz plc (AIM: KRPZ) announces that in compliance with the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules that, as at the date of this announcement, it has 1,647,367,866 Ordinary Shares, with each share carrying the right to one vote. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury.
The total voting rights figure is therefore 1,647,367,866 and may be used by shareholders (and others with notification obligations) as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

Kropz plc (AIM: KRPZ), an emerging African phosphate producer, is pleased to announce that it has received approval from the South African Reserve Bank ("

Consequently, the Fundraising is now wholly unconditional.
An application has been made for the 79,776,625 new Ordinary Shares ("New Ordinary Shares") pursuant to the Fundraising to be admitted to trading on AIM ("Admission"). Dealings in respect of the New Ordinary Shares are expected to commence on or around 8.00 a.m. on 6 May 2026.

latest.title: Operational Update
latest.content:
Today 08:44
RNS Number : 1144B
Kropz PLC
20 April 2026
20 April 2026
Kropz Plc
("
Kropz
" or the "
Company
")
Operational Update
Kropz Plc (AIM: KRPZ), an emerging African phosphate producer and developer, announces a trading update for the period to 31 March 2026.
Elandsfontein
The quarter and financial period closed with a record production month at Elandsfontein in March 2026, during which 40,792 tonnes of phosphate concentrate were produced.
As previously announced on 24 November 2025, September 2025 marked a production record of 34,070 tonnes. Since that announcement, four additional production records have been achieved during the final six months of the financial period ended 31 March 2026.
Elandsfontein produced 115,686 tonnes of phosphate concentrate in Q4 of the financial period ended 31 March 2026, representing a 11% increase compared to the previous quarter. Sales for the period totalled 104,900 tonnes.
The mine remains in its trial production phase.
Hinda Project
The Hinda Project has performed a review and assessment of alternative production scenarios, which involved comprehensive stress testing and other additional studies. As a result, the complexities associated with both surface and underground water have been significantly reduced and effectively mitigates the risk associated through optimisation initiative. Therefore, management no longer considers water-related challenges to pose a material risk, contributing to improved overall project profitability. This is a major milestone and repositions the entire feasibility of the project.

company_30s.what_they_do: KRPZ is a small mining company that extracts and sells phosphate, a key ingredient for fertilizers and various industrial products.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Phosphate is a scarce resource with steady global demand from the agriculture sector. The company owns a mine with a 35‑year life and a 10% free float, giving it a long‑term supply position that could be valuable as crop‑production needs rise.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still working through a heavy debt burden, having secured a new loan facility and a bridge loan. It is in the process of finalising its latest audited results and is exploring additional equity or debt to shore up cash flow. The stock trades very cheaply, far below what a stable mine would suggest.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy tightening its balance sheet – new loan facilities, bridge loans, and a recent wrap offer show it’s actively seeking liquidity.
Management is also pushing to keep the mine running by securing operational approvals and finalising voting rights, signalling a shift from survival to stability.
The latest filings indicate that the firm is preparing for its next quarterly results and is looking for a potential supply contract that could bring in revenue.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but cautious optimism – only a handful of traders are watching, and most are waiting for a clear sign of financial health.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few bullish posts highlight the mine’s long life and the rising global demand for phosphate, suggesting that the company could be undervalued once debt is managed.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the heavy debt load, the lack of recent revenue, and the risk that the company could default or be forced to sell assets at a discount.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has been flat for months. The recent buzz is still low‑volume, but a sudden funding announcement or a contract win would likely spark a sharp rally.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat – no public search activity, which means a news event could catch the market off‑guard.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming audited results (expected in September) could confirm that the mine is producing and that cash flow is improving.
A potential supply contract with a large fertilizer producer would create a revenue stream and give the market confidence.
If the company successfully closes its new loan facility, the influx of capital could stop the current cash burn and allow the stock to move.
A noticeable spike in trading volume above the 20‑day moving average would suggest that traders are starting to take notice.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a clear volume breakout, a price bounce to the 1.5–1.6p level reported by some posts, and any announcement of a new financing round or contract. These signals would confirm that the company is moving out of capitulation.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early phase of a deep crash recovery. The APEX compression score suggests a tight range, so a breakout could happen soon if the catalysts materialise.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The share price is roughly 86% below its peak, which is a classic deep‑crash scenario that historically delivers large rebounds. If the company stabilises its finances and the mine starts generating revenue, the price could climb to the 1.6p level that traders have mentioned – a 220% upside from the current level, and even higher if it reaches 3p. The mean‑reversion thesis is strong because the mine’s fundamentals remain intact while the market has over‑reacted to debt concerns.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that the company cannot raise enough capital to stay afloat. If the new loan or equity raise fails, the firm could default or be forced to sell assets at a discount, which would wipe out the remaining upside. A sudden drop in phosphate prices or a regulatory hurdle could also stall the mine’s production.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 300‑600% if the price reaches 1.6–3p. Potential downside: limited to the current price (0.5p or less). The asymmetry is roughly 6‑12 to 1, which is attractive for a contrarian trade.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when volume spikes and the price clears the 20‑day moving average, ideally around 1.5p. Size the position so that a 10% drop would trigger a stop. Keep an eye on the next quarterly results for confirmation.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the audited results are released or a new supply contract is announced. A price bounce to the 1.5p support level would be a clear confirmation.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – no imminent delisting or bankruptcy filings have been reported.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Deep crash, debt‑stabilisation on the horizon, and high upside if the mine starts generating cash – a contrarian bullish play.
